chunks.text:
# Retail Banking Business Glossary

This document defines the core business concepts and terminology used in the retail banking domain.

---

## Account

**Definition:** A financial arrangement between a customer and the bank that allows deposit, withdrawal, and management of funds.

**Business Context:** Accounts are the primary vehicle for customer banking relationships. They can be individual or jointly held, and may be organized into hierarchical portfolio structures for relationship managem... [TRUNCATED]

## Transaction

**Definition:** Any financial activity that affects the balance of an account, including debits, credits, transfers, withdrawals, and fees.

**Business Context:** Transactions represent the operational heartbeat of banking. They flow through multiple states (Pending → Posted) and create audit trails for regulatory compliance and customer service.

**Key Attributes:**
- Transaction ID (unique identifier)
- Transaction Type (Debit, Credit, Transfer, Payment, Withdrawal, Deposit, Fe... [TRUNCATED]

## Interest

**Definition:** The cost of borrowing money (paid by borrower) or the return on depositing money (paid by bank), expressed as an annual percentage rate.

**Business Context:** Interest is the fundamental profit mechanism in banking. Banks pay interest on deposits (lower rate) and charge interest on loans (higher rate), profiting from the spread.

**Key Attributes:**
- Interest Rate (annual percentage)
- APR (Annual Percentage Rate) - for loans
- APY (Annual Percentage Yield) - for d... [TRUNCATED]

## ATM

**Definition:** Automated Teller Machine - a self-service banking terminal that allows customers to perform basic transactions without teller assistance.

**Business Context:** ATMs extend banking access beyond branch hours and locations. They can be physically attached to branches or deployed as standalone units in high-traffic areas like malls and grocery stores.

**Key Attributes:**
- ATM ID and Code
- Location (address and GPS coordinates)
- ATM Type (Standalone, Branch, DriveThrough... [TRUNCATED]

# Retail Banking Data Dictionary

This document provides comprehensive schema documentation for the retail banking database, including table structures, column definitions, constraints, and business concept mappings.

---

## customers

**Description:** Core customer dimension table containing master data for all individual and corporate banking customers. Includes KYC (Know Your Customer) compliance status, risk profiling for investment suitability, and preferred customer flags for VIP treatmen... [TRUNCATED]

## branches

**Description:** Physical bank locations including full-service branches, satellite offices, and ATM-only locations. Tracks staffing levels, operating hours, ATM counts, and branch status for operational management.

**Primary Key:** `branch_id` (INTEGER, auto-increment)

**Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| branch_id | INTEGER | Unique branch identifier (primary key) | NO |
| branch_code | VARCHAR(4) | 4-digit ... [TRUNCATED]

## atms

**Description:** ATM locations including branch-attached machines, drive-through ATMs, and standalone units. Tracks cash balances for replenishment scheduling, GPS coordinates for location services, and operational status for availability management.

**Primary Key:** `atm_id` (INTEGER, auto-increment)

**Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| atm_id | INTEGER | Unique ATM identifier (primary key) | NO |
| atm_code | VA... [TRUNCATED]

## accounts

**Description:** Deposit account master table supporting multiple account types (checking, savings, money market, CD, investment). Implements parent-child hierarchy for portfolio aggregation (investment accounts contain child accounts). Tracks balances, interest accrual, fees, and account lifecycle status.

**Primary Key:** `account_id` (INTEGER, auto-increment)

**Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| account_id |... [TRUNCATED]

## customer_account

**Description:** Junction table implementing many-to-many relationship between customers and accounts. Supports joint ownership, authorized signers, and custodial relationships. Tracks ownership percentages and primary owner designation for billing and legal purposes.

**Primary Key:** Composite (`customer_id`, `account_id`)

**Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| customer_id | INTEGER | Foreign key to cus... [TRUNCATED]

## transactions

**Description:** Transaction fact table recording all account activity. Supports multiple transaction types including debits, credits, transfers, and withdrawals. Tracks calculated fields (balance_after) and includes location data for POS and ATM transactions for fraud detection.

**Primary Key:** `transaction_id` (INTEGER, auto-increment)

**Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| transaction_id | INTEGER | Uniq... [TRUNCATED]

## loans

**Description:** Loan portfolio table tracking all lending products including mortgages, personal loans, auto loans, HELOCs, and credit cards. Records original terms, amortization schedules, current balances, and maturity dates for loan servicing and portfolio management.

**Primary Key:** `loan_id` (INTEGER, auto-increment)

**Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| loan_id | INTEGER | Unique loan identifier (primary k... [TRUNCATED]

## cards

**Description:** Payment card issuance table for debit, credit, and ATM cards. Links cards to both customer and account. Tracks security features (chip, contactless, PIN), spending limits, and card status. Supports multiple networks (Visa, Mastercard, Discover, American Express).

**Primary Key:** `card_id` (INTEGER, auto-increment)

**Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| card_id | INTEGER | Unique card identifier (p... [TRUNCATED]

## Entity Relationship Summary

**Key Relationships:**
- customers ←→ accounts (many-to-many via customer_account)
- accounts → accounts (self-reference for parent-child hierarchy)
- accounts → transactions (one-to-many)
- customers → loans (one-to-many)
- accounts → cards (one-to-many)
- branches → accounts (one-to-many)
- branches → atms (one-to-many)

**Cascade Rules:**
- All foreign keys have NO CASCADE by default (manual integrity management)
- customer_account junction table requires both ... [TRUNCATED]

entities_post_resolution.name: Apr
entities_post_resolution.definition: A mortgage loan offer of $350,000 at 5.50% APR for 360 months with a monthly payment of $1,987.57.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: - Mortgage: $350,000 at 5.50% APR for 360 months ($1,987.57/month) | - Personal Loan: $15,000 at 8.50% APR for 48 months ($368.22/month) | - Auto Loan: $35,000 at 6.50% APR for 60 months ($684.57/month)

entities_post_resolution.name: 0.25% Apy
entities_post_resolution.definition: The annual percentage yield offered for a Money Market deposit account, tiered by balance.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: - Standard Savings: 0.25% APY (0.10% stated rate with monthly compounding) | - Premium Savings: 0.50% APY (0.495% stated rate with daily compounding) | - Money Market: 0.75% APY tiered by balance

entities_post_resolution.name: 5.50% Fixed Apr
entities_post_resolution.definition: The fixed annual percentage rate (APR) applied to a mortgage loan, here specified as 5.50% for a 360-month term.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: - Mortgage: 5.50% fixed APR for 360-month term | - 12-Month CD: 4.50% APR (4.60% APY with monthly compounding)

entities_post_resolution.name: Atm Locations
entities_post_resolution.definition: ATM cards are payment cards that enable cash withdrawals at Automated Teller Machines (ATMs) and are not used at POS terminals.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
## atms

**Description:** ATM locations including branch-attached machines, drive-through ATMs, and standalone units. Tracks cash balances for replenishment scheduling, GPS coordinates for location services, and operational status for availability management. | Definition: Automated Teller Machine - a self-service banking terminal that allows customers to perform basic transactions without teller assistance. | Synonyms: Cash Machine, Cashpoint, Automated Banking Machine | atm_daily_limit | DECIMAL(10,2) | Maximum daily ATM withdrawal | NO | Business Rules:
- ATM cards only work at ATMs (not POS terminals) | Business Context: ATMs extend banking access beyond branch hours and locations. | They can be physically attached to branches or deployed as standalone units in high-traffic areas like malls and grocery stores.

entities_post_resolution.name: Atm Type
entities_post_resolution.definition: The attribute representing the deployment type of an ATM, indicating whether it is standalone, located at a branch, or a drive-through facility.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| atm_type | VARCHAR(20) | Type: Standalone/Branch/DriveThrough | NO |
 | Key Attributes:
- ATM Type (Standalone, Branch, DriveThrough)

entities_post_resolution.name: Bank Account
entities_post_resolution.definition: A bank account is a financial arrangement between a customer and the bank that enables deposits, withdrawals, and management of funds, and serves as the primary vehicle for the customer banking relationship.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Definition: A financial arrangement between a customer and the bank that allows deposit, withdrawal, and management of funds. | Accounts are the primary vehicle for customer banking relationships. | They can be individual or jointly held, and may be organized into hierarchical portfolio structures for relationship management purposes. | - Account numbers must be unique across the system | - Account numbers must be unique | **Primary Key:** `account_id` (INTEGER, auto-increment) | **Column Details:**
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| transaction_id | INTEGER | Unique transaction identifier (primary key) | NO |
| account_id | INTEGER | Foreign key to accounts | NO | | **Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| account_id | INTEGER | Foreign key to accounts (optional, for linked accounts) | YES | | account_id | INTEGER | Foreign key to accounts (linked account) | NO | **

entities_post_resolution.name: Account Type
entities_post_resolution.definition: The account_type field identifies the classification or type of a financial account, such as Checking, Savings, Money Market, CD, or Investment.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Column Details:**
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| account_type | VARCHAR(20) | Type: Checking/Savings/MoneyMarket/CD/Investment | NO | | - Account Type (Checking, Savings, MoneyMarket, CD, Investment)

entities_post_resolution.name: Account Status
entities_post_resolution.definition: Account Status is the current state of an account in the information system, indicating activity level and usability (e.g., Active, Dormant, Frozen, Closed, Blocked, Expired).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
- Account Status (Active, Dormant, Frozen, Closed) | **Column Details:**
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| status | VARCHAR(20) | Status: Active/Dormant/Frozen/Closed | NO |
 | status | VARCHAR(20) | Status: Active/Frozen/Blocked/Expired | NO

entities_post_resolution.name: Amount
entities_post_resolution.definition: A monetary value representing an amount of money in a given currency, such as principal amount or periodic payment amount, used in financial transactions.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
- Amount and Currency | - Principal Amount | - Monthly Payment Amount | - Balance Due | **Column Details:**
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| amount | DECIMAL(15,2) | Transaction amount (always positive) | NO |

entities_post_resolution.name: Annual Percentage Yield
entities_post_resolution.definition: Annual Percentage Rate (APR) is the annual rate charged for borrowing money, representing the cost of credit on a yearly basis.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
- APY (Annual Percentage Yield) - for deposits | - APR (Annual Percentage Rate) - for loans | **Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| interest_rate | DECIMAL(5,4) | Annual interest rate (APR) | NO |

entities_post_resolution.name: Available Balance
entities_post_resolution.definition: The amount of funds in an account that are immediately accessible for withdrawal or use, after accounting for holds or pending transactions.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Default Values:**
- available_balance: 0.00 | - Available balance may be less than current balance due to holds or pending transactions

entities_post_resolution.name: Balance Due
entities_post_resolution.definition: The current outstanding balance owed on an account or invoice.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| balance_due | DECIMAL(15,2) | Current outstanding balance | NO | | - Balance Due

entities_post_resolution.name: Branch
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Branch is a physical banking location of a bank where customers can access in-person services, identified by attributes such as branch_id, branch_code, branch_name, and branch_type.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
## Branch

**Definition:** A physical banking location where customers can access in-person services including teller transactions, account opening, loan applications, and financial advisory services. | **Definition:** A physical banking location where customers can access in-person services including teller transactions, account opening, loan applications, and financial advisory services. | - Branch codes must be unique | **Business Context:** Branches represent the physical presence of the bank. They range from full-service headquarters with multiple staff to satellite locations with limited services and ATM-only locations with no staff. | **Column Details:**
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| branch_id | INTEGER | Unique branch identifier (primary key) | NO |
| branch_code | VARCHAR(4) | 4-digit branch code, must be unique | NO | | UNIQUE (branch_code) | Indexes:
- idx_branches_code ON (branch_code) | Primary Key: `branch_id` (INT

entities_post_resolution.name: Current Timestamp
entities_post_resolution.definition: A default value function that yields the current date and time to auto-populate timestamp fields (for example, created_at) in records.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Default Values:
- created_at: CURRENT_TIMESTAMP | **Default Values:**
- created_at: CURRENT_TIMESTAMP | **Default Values:**
- updated_at: CURRENT_TIMESTAMP | **Column Details:**
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| transaction_date | TIMESTAMP | When transaction occurred | NO |
 | **Column Details:**
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| created_at | TIMESTAMP | Record creation timestamp | NO |
 | Default Values:
- opened_date: CURRENT_DATE | **Default Values:**
- opened_date: CURRENT_DATE

entities_post_resolution.name: Payment Card
entities_post_resolution.definition: A payment card issued to a customer, linked to their bank account or line of credit, enabling electronic transactions at POS terminals, ATMs, and online merchants.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Synonyms: Payment Card, Bank Card, Plastic | - Card numbers must be unique | card_id | INTEGER | Unique card identifier (primary key) | NO | Primary Key: `card_id` (INTEGER, auto-increment) | FOREIGN KEY (account_id) REFERENCES accounts(account_id) | FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(customer_id) | card_name | VARCHAR(50) | Cardholder name as printed on card | NO | card_number | VARCHAR(20) | Primary Account Number (PAN), must be unique | NO | card_type | VARCHAR(20) | Type: Debit/Credit/ATM | NO | Definition: A payment card issued to a customer, linked to their bank account or credit line, enabling electronic transactions at POS terminals, ATMs, and online merchants. | Business Context: Cards are the primary interface for customer banking activities. They range from ATM-only cards to full-featured debit cards and revolving credit cards, each with specific security features and spending limits. | Key Attributes:
- Card ID and Card Number (PAN)
- Card Type (Debit, Credit, A

entities_post_resolution.name: Payment Card
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Payment Card is a financial instrument (Debit/Credit/ATM) represented in the system by a unique internal ID, a Primary Account Number (PAN), the cardholder name printed on the card, and a card type.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Synonyms: Payment Card, Bank Card, Plastic | - Card numbers must be unique | card_id | INTEGER | Unique card identifier (primary key) | NO | Primary Key: `card_id` (INTEGER, auto-increment) | FOREIGN KEY (account_id) REFERENCES accounts(account_id) | FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(customer_id) | card_name | VARCHAR(50) | Cardholder name as printed on card | NO | card_number | VARCHAR(20) | Primary Account Number (PAN), must be unique | NO | card_type | VARCHAR(20) | Type: Debit/Credit/ATM | NO

entities_post_resolution.name: Cardless Withdrawals
entities_post_resolution.definition: The capability of enabling withdrawals without a physical card (cardless withdrawals), including related authentication and ATM support aspects.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Cardless transactions require mobile app authentication | **Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| supports_cardless | BOOLEAN | Whether ATM supports mobile/cardless withdrawals | NO |

entities_post_resolution.name: Cash Balance
entities_post_resolution.definition: The amount of physical cash currently available (on hand) for a given entity/location, used to monitor liquidity and schedule replenishment.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| cash_balance | DECIMAL(12,2) | Current cash on hand | YES |
 | Key Attributes:
- Cash Balance and Last Replenished Date | ## atms

**Description:** ATM locations including branch-attached machines, drive-through ATMs, and standalone units. Tracks cash balances for replenishment scheduling, GPS coordinates for location services, and operational status for availability management.

entities_post_resolution.name: Savings Money Market Cd
entities_post_resolution.definition: A consolidated set of bank account types offered by a financial institution, consisting of Checking, Savings, MoneyMarket, CD (certificate of deposit), and Investment accounts.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
- Account Type (Checking, Savings, MoneyMarket, CD, Investment) | **Column Details:**
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| account_type | VARCHAR(20) | Type: Checking/Savings/MoneyMarket/CD/Investment | NO |

entities_post_resolution.name: Contactless
entities_post_resolution.definition: The capability for contactless (tap-to-pay) payments in the system, including any transaction limits and whether the feature is enabled.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Business Rules:
- Contactless (tap-to-pay) has transaction amount limits | contactless_enabled | BOOLEAN | Whether tap-to-pay is enabled | NO

entities_post_resolution.name: Credit Card
entities_post_resolution.definition: A credit card is a revolving line of credit issued by a financial institution that allows the holder to borrow up to a specified limit and repay with interest over time, typically with monthly payments.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
- Credit Card: $10,000 limit at 18.50% APR, $250 minimum monthly payment | - Credit Card: 18.50% variable APR based on prime rate | - Credit card loans have no fixed term (revolving credit) | Business Rules:
- Credit cards access revolving credit line requiring monthly payments

entities_post_resolution.name: Credit Transactions
entities_post_resolution.definition: Credit transactions are accounting entries that increase the balance of an account, representing the credit side of transactions in financial records.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: - Credit transactions increase account balance | - accounts → transactions (one-to-many)

entities_post_resolution.name: Currency Code
entities_post_resolution.definition: Three-letter ISO currency code used to denote the monetary currency for amounts and transactions.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
- Amount and Currency | **Column Details:**
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| currency | VARCHAR(3) | Currency code (default: USD) | NO |
 | **Default Values:**
- currency: 'USD'
- transaction_date: CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
- status: 'Pending'
- created_at: CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Customer is an individual or corporate entity that maintains a banking relationship with the institution and undergoes Know Your Customer (KYC) verification.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Definition: An individual or corporate entity that maintains a banking relationship with the institution, having completed Know Your Customer (KYC) verification. | Each customer undergoes KYC screening to comply with regulatory requirements and is assigned a risk profile to guide product recommendations and investment strategies. | **Business Concept Mapping:** Maps to **Customer** business glossary concept | Business Rules:
- Expired cards are automatically renewed and mailed to customer | **Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| customer_id | INTEGER | Foreign key to customers (borrower) | NO | | FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(customer_id) | customer_id | INTEGER | Foreign key to customers (cardholder) | NO | ## Entity Relationship Summary

**Key Relationships:**
- customers ←→ accounts (many-to-many via customer_account) | - customers → loans (one-to-many)

entities_post_resolution.name: Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: A date attribute representing key lifecycle dates for a financial instrument or account, specifically the origination date and maturity date.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: issued_date | DATE | Date card was issued | NO | - Origination Date and Maturity Date

entities_post_resolution.name: Daily Limit
entities_post_resolution.definition: A per-day cap on transaction amounts applied to an account, across channels such as ATM withdrawals and POS purchases, to limit daily spending/withdrawal exposure.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
daily_limit | DECIMAL(10,2) | Maximum daily POS spending | NO | Business Rules:
- Daily limits prevent fraud and control exposure | atm_daily_limit | DECIMAL(10,2) | Maximum daily ATM withdrawal | NO

entities_post_resolution.name: Debit Transactions
entities_post_resolution.definition: Debit cards are payment instruments linked to a bank account that deduct funds directly from the linked checking account when used.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
- Debit transactions reduce account balance | Business Rules:
- Debit cards deduct directly from linked checking account

entities_post_resolution.name: Transaction Type
entities_post_resolution.definition: The transaction_type entity defines the discrete categories (Debit, Credit, Transfer, Payment, Withdrawal, Deposit, Fee) used to classify a financial transaction.
synonyms: Debit, Credit, Transfer, Payment, Withdrawal, Deposit, Fee
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Column Details:**
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| transaction_id | INTEGER | Unique transaction identifier (primary key) | NO |
| account_id | INTEGER | Foreign key to accounts | NO |
| transaction_type | VARCHAR(20) | Type: Debit/Credit/Transfer/Payment/Withdrawal/Deposit/Fee | NO |
 | **Constraints:**
- CHECK (transaction_type IN ('Debit', 'Credit', 'Transfer', 'Payment', 'Withdrawal', 'Deposit', 'Fee')) | - Transaction Type (Debit, Credit, Transfer, Payment, Withdrawal, Deposit, Fee)

entities_post_resolution.name: Interest On Deposits
entities_post_resolution.definition: Interest paid by a bank to depositors on their bank deposits, representing the yield or compensation for keeping funds in deposits.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: - Deposit interest is credited monthly (typically on first day of month) | **Business Context:** Interest is the fundamental profit mechanism in banking. Banks pay interest on deposits (lower rate) and charge interest on loans (higher rate), profiting from the spread.

entities_post_resolution.name: Transaction Description
entities_post_resolution.definition: A textual field in a transaction record that contains the human-readable description of the transaction (often stored as a column named 'description').
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
- Description and Reference Number | **Column Details:**
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| description | VARCHAR(200) | Human-readable transaction description | YES |

entities_post_resolution.name: Financial Transaction
entities_post_resolution.definition: A financial transaction is any activity that affects an account balance (debits, credits, transfers, withdrawals, and fees) and flows through processing states, creating an audit trail for regulatory compliance and customer service.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Synonyms: Financial Transaction, Account Activity, Bank Transaction | Definition: Any financial activity that affects the balance of an account, including debits, credits, transfers, withdrawals, and fees. | They flow through multiple states (Pending → Posted) and create audit trails for regulatory compliance and customer service. | ## transactions

**Description:** Transaction fact table recording all account activity. Supports multiple transaction types including debits, credits, transfers, and withdrawals. Tracks calculated fields (balance_after) and includes location data for POS and ATM transactions for fraud detection. | - Transactions must maintain sufficient funds (except for overdraft-protected accounts)

entities_post_resolution.name: Gps Coordinates Latitude
entities_post_resolution.definition: Geographic coordinates (latitude and longitude) used to uniquely identify a physical location for assets, services, and location-based information.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
## atms

**Description:** ATM locations including branch-attached machines, drive-through ATMs, and standalone units. Tracks cash balances for replenishment scheduling, GPS coordinates for location services, and operational status for availability management. | **Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| latitude | DECIMAL(10,6) | GPS latitude coordinate | YES |
 | **Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| longitude | DECIMAL(11,6) | GPS longitude coordinate | YES |
 | Key Attributes:
- Location (address and GPS coordinates)

entities_post_resolution.name: Status
entities_post_resolution.definition: The status attribute's allowed values representing the lifecycle states of a process or transaction, specifically Pending, Posted, Failed, Cancelled, and OnHold.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
- Status (Pending, Posted, Failed, Cancelled, OnHold) | Key Attributes:
- Status (Operational, OutOfService, OutOfCash) | **Constraints:**
- CHECK (status IN ('Pending', 'Posted', 'Failed', 'Cancelled', 'OnHold'))

entities_post_resolution.name: Atm Installation Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: The calendar date on which an ATM was installed.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Key Attributes:
- Installation Date | **Column Details:**

| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|

Banks pay interest on deposits (lower rate) and charge interest on loans (higher rate), profiting from the spread.

tables_enriched.name: Customers
tables_enriched.description: Stores customer identity and contact details, verification status, risk profile, and timestamps for when the customer was created and last updated.

tables_enriched.name: Branches
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about bank branches, including identification, location, contact details, operating hours, counts of ATMs and tellers, leadership, opening date, status, and record creation timestam... [TRUNCATED]

tables_enriched.name: ATMS
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about ATMs, including location, status, cash balance, capabilities, and timestamps for installation and last replenishment.

tables_enriched.name: Accounts
tables_enriched.description: Stores customer account records including balances, status, and transactional activity for each account, along with hierarchical relationships to parent accounts and branch associations.

tables_enriched.name: Customer Account
tables_enriched.description: Stores the associations between customers and accounts, including the type of relationship, whether the account is the primary one for the customer, ownership share, and timestamps for when the linkag... [TRUNCATED]

tables_enriched.name: Transactions
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of financial transactions for accounts, including amounts, currencies, dates, statuses, and reference details for auditing and reconciliation.

tables_enriched.name: Loans
tables_enriched.description: Stores loan records including borrower, loan terms, payment details, and lifecycle dates for each loan.

tables_enriched.name: CARDS
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about customer cards, including identifiers, card metadata, security features, usage limits, status, and timestamps for issuance and activity.
